exemplar.title:
How does development of public transport,
specifically the train station Copenhagen South, in Valby,
Denmark, affect the surrounding housing market and both
the age structure and income level of the local population?

Denmark, affect the surrounding housing market and both
the age structure and income level of the local population?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic and scope are introduced with precision, reinforced by maps, tables and a clear hypothesis
Research question is explicitly stated, tightly delimited and consistently referenced throughout
Overall communication remains focused and effective, with clear geographic and thematic emphasis
weaknesses: Methodology lacks detail on time-series processing and robust statistical testing
Primary survey sample of only seven estate agents is small and under-justified
Repetition of the research question in the introduction creates unnecessary redundancy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic clarity and question formulation) meet the top band, while the methodology is only âmostly completeâ (band 3-4). A holistic score of 5 reflects the very strong focus and clarity, tempered by methodological limitations.

strengths: Demonstrates solid subject knowledge of gentrification theory and urban demographics
Effective conceptual model grounds the theoretical context
Terminology (e.g. correlation coefficient, demographic categories) is used correctly and consistently
weaknesses: Use of sources remains largely descriptive with limited critical comparison
Footnote formatting is inconsistent and citations are sometimes redundant
Minor spelling and labelling slips (e.g. âinfastructureâ, age-category inconsistency)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge is clear with mostly effective use of sources and terminology. Performance sits squarely in the band 3-4, warranting a score of 4.

strengths: Data selection and control areas show clear relevance to the research question
Quantitative and qualitative interpretations (e.g. Pearson r, spider diagrams) are mostly well supported
Argument is coherent, returning to the hypothesis and theoretical model throughout
weaknesses: Coverage of confounding factors is limited and causal language isnât always qualified
No statistical significance test accompanies the Pearson coefficient
Minor inconsistencies and over-assertions appear in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research application and argumentation reach the 7-9 band; analysis is good with mostly supported conclusions (band 4-6). Overall, the performance is stronger than borderline, meriting an 8 out of 12.

strengths: Structure follows IB EE conventions with numbered headings and a comprehensive table of contents
Pagination, headings and sequencing support reader navigation
weaknesses: Some figures are low resolution and captions repeat or omit axis titles
Table numbering is inconsistent and certain layout elements (page references, cell formatting) need correction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure meets the top band, but layout issues place presentation in the middle. A score of 3 reflects this mix of strong organization and partial lapses in layout.

strandSelections.reasoning: Data chosen are clearly relevant to the RQ; controls and temporal span enhance validity. The student consistently links evidence to the transport intervention, showing good application, though coverage of confounding factors is limited. This sustained relevance meets the lower end of the 7-9 band.

A mixed-methods design (secondary national statistics + small primary survey) is explained together with justification for control areas. However sampling of only seven estate agents, limited detail on time-series processing and lack of explanation of statistical tests beyond one Pearson value indicate only âmostly completeâ rather than fully appropriate coverage. This aligns with the 3-4 band.

The essay demonstrates solid understanding of gentrification theory, transport geography and urban demographic change and applies these accurately to Copenhagen. Sources are used, but often descriptive and with limited critical comparison, preventing the depth expected for âexcellentâ. Thus the work fits the âclear knowledgeâ¦ partially effective use of sourcesâ band.

Pagination, headings, images and tables are generally neat, but some figures have low resolution, captions occasionally repeat, and inconsistent font coding appears in maths. These partial lapses show the layout is only âpartially appliedâ, suiting band 1-2.

Key geographical terms (gentrification stages, demographic categories, correlation coefficient) are used correctly and consistently in most sections; minor slips (e.g. âinfAsTructureâ, inconsistent age-category labelling) prevent a flawless record. Overall usage is âmostly accurateâ, placing the work in band 3-4.

The student introduces gentrification, the spatial focus (Valby / Copenhagen South-west) and the intended variables with precision in the first pages. Maps, tables and a hypothesis reinforce a clear sense of purpose and geographical focus. Throughout the essay the reader is never in doubt about what is being investigated or why, so the communication reaches the top-band descriptor.

The narrative is coherent: each section returns to the hypothesis and model of gentrification, culminating in a logical conclusion. Minor repetition and occasional over-assertion create small inconsistencies, positioning the work at the lower edge of the 7-9 âeffective argumentâ band.

Quantitative analysis (percentage change tables, Pearson r) and qualitative interpretation of survey results are present and mostly supported by data. Evaluation of limitations is thin and some causal claims lack depth. Hence the analysis is âgood with mostly supported conclusionsâ, meriting the top of the 4-6 band.

The essay follows the IB EE structure with numbered headings, clear sequencing from introduction through conclusion, and an extensive contents table that aids navigation. The structure actively supports reader understanding, matching the upper band.

The research question is explicitly stated, tightly delimited to one transport intervention, three measurable variables and two comparison areas. It is neither too broad nor vague and continually frames later analysis, satisfying the highest descriptor for focus and effectiveness.

annotations.comment: The heading â1.2 Aimsâ uses raw LaTeX tags in the HTML; update formatting to plain text for consistency.
The investigation title succinctly communicates the scope and focus of the study, immediately orienting the reader to the geographical and thematic context.
The research question is clearly stated and precisely focused on specific variables and locations, effectively guiding the entire investigation.
Including the word count at the start meets IB requirements and confirms adherence to the 4,000-word limit.
The image caption contains a typographical error (âfroundâ); please correct spelling and verify citation formatting.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-structured, aiding navigation through the essayâs sections.
The entry for â3. Data presentation and analysisâ lacks a page number; please update the table to include all page references.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent; ensure all citations use a consistent style and correct placement of superscripts.
Figure 1 presents a clear conceptual model of economic factors driving gentrification, effectively grounding the theoretical context.
The research question is restated verbatim in the introduction, creating unnecessary repetition. Consider referencing it more succinctly.
The Pearson correlation is reported with excessive precision; round to two decimal places for readability.
The survey discussion of transport importance would benefit from error bars or a measure of variability.
This paragraph runs on with multiple clauses and parentheticals; consider splitting into shorter sentences for clarity.
The description of control areas is clear and shows strong relevance to the research question and methodological design.
Multiple footnotes cite the same source for Laursen (2022); consolidate these into a single reference to avoid redundancy.
Table 1âs header text breaks awkwardly across lines; adjust cell formatting to improve readability.
The map discussion does not specify axis titles or coordinate reference; include these in captions for clarity.
The alternative hypothesis is stated too generally; specify which confounding variables will be measured to test it.
The sample of seven real-estate agents is small; consider justifying its representativeness or expanding the survey.
There is a misspelling âinfastructureâ in the analysis; ensure accurate use of geographical terminology.
When interpreting the price graph, explicitly reference key data points and dates to strengthen analytical clarity.
Table numbering appears inconsistent (Table 2 vs Table 3); verify and correct numbering throughout.
The discussion of timing demonstrates insightful connection between station phases and price acceleration.
Figures 2 and 3 present age distributions with clear legends and consistent styling.
The demographic inference is well supported by percentage increases in the 21â30 age cohort.
No statistical significance test accompanies the Pearson coefficient; consider adding a p-value or confidence interval.
Causal language appears in income analysis without discussing potential confounders; address other influencing factors.
The narrative on price increase reasons ends mid-sentence; revise to complete the thought and maintain coherence.
Figure 9 is styled as a section header rather than a caption; use consistent caption styling to avoid confusion.
Spider diagrams in Figure 10 effectively illustrate qualitative shifts in buyer profiles, enriching the argument.
The conclusion section is interwoven with figures; consider separating narrative text and visuals to reinforce structure.
The conclusion aptly revisits aims and synthesizes findings, demonstrating coherent subject knowledge.
